meta.role: CX Learning & Development Facilitation & Programs Manager

header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Own {coord|end-to-end} implementation of 20+ {prog|learning programs}, driving continuous improvement via data and feedback for measurable outcomes
Facilitate live {prog|cohort-based training} and webinars for 600+ participants, driving understanding and a 78% program enrollment rate
Conduct needs assessments with 25 global {cross|stakeholders} to identify learning gaps, designing 3 new programs best aligned with needs
Develop guides and {prog|learning materials} using {data|AI tools} for 70+ staff, reducing onboarding time by 75%
Upskill the client team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
Empower stakeholders to present executive-ready insights on $362K in costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed L&D strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights and recommending actions to the C-suite on engagement
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|learner experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Directed a Merger & Acquisition project, migrating and merging employee records across systems
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation

projects: Consulting Projects: Informed Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping engagement for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: L&D & Program Tools: LMS (Canvas, Moodle) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · Knowledge Management (SharePoint, Notion)
Data & Systems: Data Analysis (Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS) · HRIS/SaaS Admin (SAP, Rippling, ADP) · ATS (Greenhouse)
Productivity: AI Tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, prompt engineering) · Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Excel) · Google Workspace
Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)

jd.meta: Gusto — CX Learning & Development Facilitation & Programs Manager

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Gusto — CX L&D Facilitation & Programs Manager (Denver primary, multi-city banding → Portland header, open to relocation). Summary → Learning & Development, systematic approaches. InGenius → Set A: end-to-end program implementation + measurable outcomes, cohort-based facilitation + 78% enrollment, needs assessments for learning gaps, learning materials via AI tools. Reordered to lead with program implementation. ODN → Pair A (quantified results + training). NextGen → L&D-strategy framing. Vestas → led with Inclusive Leadership workshop (facilitation). Skills → led with LMS + PM, added prompt engineering (JD AI fluency), kept HRCI. NOTE: JD wants 5+ yrs L&D + people-management of facilitators; Jamie has L&D + facilitation but limited direct people-management — truthful overlap is strong on facilitation/program-management/AI; manager-of-team is a stretch.
